user_goal: Develop a 3D Crossy Road game within a single HTML file utilizing Three.js via CDN
original_user_request: Develop a 3D Crossy Road game within a single HTML file utilizing Three.js via CDN

intent_contract.original_user_request: Develop a 3D Crossy Road game within a single HTML file utilizing Three.js via CDN
intent_contract.precise_rewrite: Create an HTML file that includes the necessary Three.js library from a CDN and implements a 3D Crossy Road game.
assumptions: The user wants a fully functional 3D game in a single HTML file.
Three.js should be used for rendering the game.

tradeoffs.name: Speed vs Quality
tradeoffs.rationale: Balancing between development speed and ensuring a high-quality game experience.

tradeoffs.name: Realism vs Fun
tradeoffs.rationale: Deciding whether to prioritize realism in the game design or creating an engaging, fun gameplay experience.

tradeoffs.name: Security vs Cost
tradeoffs.rationale: Evaluating the trade-off between ensuring the game is secure and minimizing costs.

speed.rationale: The user likely wants a quick development process to get the game up and running.

quality.rationale: A high-quality game experience is important, but speed is also crucial.

realism.rationale: Realism is a factor, but the primary goal seems to be creating an engaging game rather than a photorealistic one.

fun.rationale: The user specifically mentions fun, indicating that enjoyment should be a top priority.

security.rationale: Security is important but may not be the highest priority given the constraints of a single HTML file and CDN usage.

cost.rationale: Cost should be considered, but it's likely that free resources like CDN-hosted libraries will suffice.

definition_of_done: The HTML file is created and includes the necessary Three.js library from a CDN.
A basic 3D Crossy Road game is implemented within the HTML file.
The game runs in a web browser without errors.
The game provides an engaging and fun gameplay experience.
non_goals: Implementing advanced security features beyond basic CDN usage.
Exceeding the budget for resources or development time.
conflict_rules: In case of conflicts between speed and quality, prioritize quality to ensure a good user experience.
If realism is compromised for fun, focus on creating an engaging game that still meets the user's expectations.
Security should be considered but not at the expense of other priorities. If security becomes a blocking issue, re-evaluate the project requirements.

file_excerpts.content:
{
  "name": "@hivo/agent-runtime",
  "version": "0.2.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./dist/index.js",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "tsx watch --clear-screen=false src/index.ts",
    "build": "tsc -p tsconfig.json",
    "typecheck": "tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit",
    "test": "npm run build && node --test dist/tests/*.test.js",
    "test:reasoning-v2": "npm run build && node --test dist/tests/adaptive-reasoning-kernel.test.js dist/tests/adaptive-reasoning-certification.test.js dist/tests/reasoning-architecture.test.js dist/tests/decision-pipeline.test.js",
    "memory:index": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts index repo --workspace ../..",
    "memory:index-status": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts index status --workspace ../..",
    "memory:index-refresh": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts index refresh --workspace ../..",
    "memory:index-explain": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts index explain --workspace ../..",
    "memory:inspect": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory inspect --workspace ../..",
    "memory:status": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory status --workspace ../..",
    "memory:show-commands": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory show-commands --workspace ../..",
    "memory:clean-runs": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory clean-runs --workspace ../..",
    "memory:compact": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory compact --workspace ../..",
    "memory:lessons": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory lessons --workspace ../..",
    "memory:decisions": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory decisions --workspace ../..",
    "memory:failed-attempts": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory failed-attempts --workspace ../..",
    "memory:explain-task": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory explain-task --workspace ../..",
    "memory:migrate-sqlite": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory migrate-sqlite --workspace ../..",
    "memory:export-backup": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory export-backup --workspace ../..",
    "memory:search": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory search --workspace ../..",
    "memory:db-status": "tsx src/memory/cli.ts memory db-status --workspace ../..",
    "agent:run": "tsx src/orchestration/cli.ts agent run --workspace ../..",
    "agent:plan": "tsx src/orchestration/cli.ts agent plan --workspace ../..",
    "agent:inspect-run": "tsx src/orchestration/cli.ts agent inspect-run --workspace ../..",
    "agent:report": "tsx src/orchestration/cli.ts agent report --workspace ../..",
    "agent:resume": "tsx src/orchestration/cli.ts agent resume --workspace ../..",
    "agent:trial:architecture-scan": "tsx src/orchestration/cli.ts agent trial architecture-scan --workspace ../..",
    "agent:trial:test-discovery": "tsx src/orchestration/cli.ts agent trial test-discovery --workspace ../..",
    "agent:trial:staffing-eval": "tsx src/orchestration/cli.ts agent trial staffing-eval --workspace ../..",
    "agent:trial:scheduler-scale": "tsx src/orchestration/cli.ts agent trial scheduler-scale --workspace ../..",
    "agent:trial:compare": "tsx src/orchestration/cli.ts agent trial compare --

constraints: Preserve the canonical original user request and intent ledger references in all derived context and output metadata.
Every successful output must include intent_alignment tied to these exact values.
No file edits, patches, diffs, or shell commands.
Validation can be recommended but not marked mechanically passed.

inclusion_metadata.inclusion_reason: Read-only file excerpts were included so provider-backed workers can inspect evidence, not just file names.
